channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Supreme Court schedules arguments in case where Trump administration is defending ACA
item.description: The Supreme Court on Monday scheduled arguments for April 21 in a case that could decide the legality of the Affordable Care Act’s (ACA) requirement that insurers cover certain preventive services.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Weixel, NewsNation

item.title: Judge declines to restore AP access to White House amid 'Gulf of America' dispute
item.description: A federal judge on Monday declined to restore The Associated Press’s access to certain spaces open to the media at the White House, including the Oval Office amid the Trump administration’s battle against the wire service over its refusal to use “Gulf of America” in its popular stylebook. U.S.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, NewsNation

item.title: Under threat of termination, VA workers return to the office
item.description: President Donald Trump has ordered federal workers to return to the office, and that’s exactly what happened at the Department of Veterans Affairs on Monday. “This is an exciting day,” VA Secretary Doug Collins said in a video posted on social media that included footage of him greeting employees.
item.dc:creator: Marc Sternfield, KTLA 5

item.title: Judge rejects immediately restoring AP's access to White House but urges government to reconsider
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A federal judge on Monday refused to immediately order the White House to restore The Associated Press' access to presidential events, saying the news organization had not demonstrated it had suffered any irreparable harm.
item.dc:creator: MATT SEDENSKY, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Trump expresses hope Russia's war in Ukraine is nearing an endgame as he meets with France's Macron
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump expressed hope that Russia's war in Ukraine is nearing an endgame as he met Monday with French President Emmanuel Macron on the third anniversary of the invasion.
item.dc:creator: SYLVIE CORBET, MATTHEW LEE and AAMER MADHANI, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Catholic bishops sue Trump over refugee resettlement suspension
item.description: “We are urging the government to uphold its legal and moral obligations to refugees,” said the executive director of public affairs of the United States Conference of Catholic Bishops.
item.dc:creator: Susie Pinto, NewsNation

item.title: Ron DeSantis talks up his wife as next Florida governor and takes a shot at Trump's pick
item.description: TALLAHASSEE, Fla. (AP) — As Florida Gov. Ron DeSantis approaches the end of his second term in office, he’s talking up one potential heir to succeed him in 2026: his wife, Casey DeSantis. And he's taking a shot at President Donald Trump 's pick to be the next governor.
item.dc:creator: KATE PAYNE, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Angry Democratic donors turn off the flow of money
item.description: Democrats are anxious to rebuild their party on the heels of President Donald Trump’s victory in November. But they have a major problem as they try to refashion their brand: The money isn’t there.
item.dc:creator: Hanna Trudo, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump posts SpongeBob meme mocking federal workers after email requesting accomplishments
item.description: President Donald Trump posted a SpongeBob meme mocking federal employees in the wake of an email sent to the workforce asking them to document their achievements in the past week.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, Fox 5

item.title: 3 arrested after more than 25 exotic cars street race in Florida
item.description: Three people were arrested after more than 25 high-performance cars were found speeding and doing donuts.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Suter, NewsNation

item.title: Trump posts SpongeBob meme mocking federal workers
item.description: Trump posted a SpongeBob meme mocking federal employees in the wake of an email sent asking them to document their achievements.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, NewsNation

item.title: 11 dead after supplemental shakes linked to listeria cases in 21 states
item.description: Frozen shakes sold to nursing homes and other institutions are being recalled after a listeria outbreak in 21 states that has caused 11 deaths, according to the Federal Food and Drug Administration.
item.dc:creator: Ryan Johnston, WGN 9

item.title: Federal workers confront mass confusion as Musk's deadline looms
item.description: Confusion and chaos loom as hundreds of thousands of federal employees begin their workweek facing a deadline from Elon Musk to explain their recent accomplishments or risk losing their jobs.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump announces Dan Bongino as Deputy FBI Director
item.description: Bongino is a former U.S. Secret Service agent that became known for his defense of Trump while previously appearing as a Fox News contributor.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Hospital patient breaks 'every bone' in nurse's face during attack: Police
item.description: A Florida man is facing attempted murder charges after allegedly attacking a nurse at a hospital where he was being cared for, essentially breaking every bone in her face.
item.dc:creator: Emily Smith, NewsNation

item.title: Bell takes only lead in OT to edge Hocevar, Larson in another thrilling NASCAR finish in Atlanta
item.description: Christopher Bell celebrated a drought-ending victory for himself and Joe Gibbs Racing after a dramatic comeback in Atlanta.
item.dc:creator: CHARLES ODUM, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: How possible USPS changes could affect your mail
item.description: “We want to have a post office that works well and doesn’t lose massive amounts of money," Trump said. “We’re thinking about doing that. And it’ll be a form of a merger, but it’ll remain the Postal Service, and I think it’ll operate a lot better.
item.dc:creator: Brad Hamilton, KTLA 5

item.title: The Trump administration is firing 2,000 USAID workers and putting thousands of others on leave
item.description: The Trump administration said Sunday that it was placing all but a fraction of staffers at the U.S. Agency for International Development on leave worldwide and eliminating 2,000 U.S.-based staff positions.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Nearly 200 Venezuelan migrants are flown home from Guantanamo Bay, with a layover in Honduras
item.description: CARACAS, Venezuela (AP) — Nearly 200 Venezuelan immigrants to the U.S. were returned to their home country after being detained at Guantanamo Bay, in a flurry of flights that forged an unprecedented pathway for U.S. deportations. U.S.
item.dc:creator: MORGAN LEE and REGINA GARCIA CANO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: New Uber feature may overcharge you. Here's how to turn it off
item.description: Unless you disable it, a new setting in the app may end up costing you the next time you hail a ride overseas.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, ABC 4

item.title: Alex Rodriguez makes half-court shot, wins Bucknell student $10,000
item.description: Former Major League Baseball star Alex Rodriguez showed his skill on the hardwood Sunday, making a half-court shot to win a Bucknell student $10,000.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: New Uber feature may overcharge you. Here's how to turn it off
item.description: Unless you disable it, a new setting in the app may end up costing you the next time you hail a ride overseas.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, KTLA 5

item.title: Motive in Pennsylvania hospital shooting unclear, but officials say man had recent contact with ICU
item.description: Investigators are trying to determine why a man targeted a Pennsylvania hospital, taking workers inside the intensive care unit hostage and holding one staff member at gunpoint as her hands were bound by zip ties.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Lynne Marie Stewart of 'Pee-wee's Playhouse,' 'It's Always Sunny' dies: reports
item.description: The actress has recently finished filming for another season of "It's Always Sunny in Philadelphia" and her forthcoming pickleball-related film, "Dink.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, Fox 5

item.title: Ukraine's Zelenskyy says he'd be ready to give up presidency if it brought peace, NATO membership
item.description: “If to achieve peace, you really need me to give up my post, I’m ready,” Zelenskyy said in response to a journalist's question on whether he’d trade his office for peace. “I can trade it for NATO.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Wealthy real estate brothers face new lawsuits as defense claims criminal investigation was orchestrated
item.description: New lawsuits against the Alexander brothers were filed, as the brothers claim a high-profile personal-injury law firm "dictated" the criminal investigation.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Lakers snap Nuggets' 9-game winning streak behind Luka Doncic's best game yet for LA
item.description: Luka Doncic scored 32 points in his best game by far with the Lakers, leading Los Angeles to a 123-100 runaway win at Denver on Saturday night that snapped the Nuggets' nine-game winning streak.
item.dc:creator: ARNIE STAPLETON, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: One dead, one injured after shooting at New Mexico Air Force base
item.description: Kirtland Air Force base said the 377th Security Forces Squadron was called to an incident at approximately 2 a.m.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, ABC 4

item.title: Federal judge temporarily blocks Trump DEI executive orders
item.description: A federal judge has temporarily blocked parts of President Trump’s executive orders that sought to end the federal government’s backing of diversity, equity and inclusion (DEI) efforts.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, WFLA, NBC

item.title: USPS honors beloved actress Betty White with commemorative stamp
item.description: The stamp features a digitally created portrait of White against a violet background with "bubbly spots that befit her sparkling personality.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, KRON4

item.title: Luigi Mangione makes first court appearance since arraignment in UnitedHealthcare CEO's death
item.description: Luigi Mangione, 26, has pleaded not guilty to multiple counts of murder, including murder as an act of terrorism, in the Dec. 4 killing of Brian Thompson outside a midtown Manhattan hotel.
item.dc:creator: Michael R. Sisak, ABC 4

item.title: IRS layoffs could hurt revenue collection and foil efforts to go after rich tax dodgers, experts say
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The layoffs of roughly 7,000 IRS probationary workers beginning this week likely mean the end of the agency's plan to go after high-wealth tax dodgers and could spell disaster for revenue collections, experts say.
item.dc:creator: FATIMA HUSSEIN, Associated Press, ABC 4
